chunks.title: Course Description
chunks.content: Fundamental basis from materials and manufacturing methods selection in engineering design; basic elements and theory of a range of materials manufacturing methods, with specific emphasis on recent developments in the field including rapid prototyping 3D, computer control of machines - tools, automation robotics micro-fabrication and metrology.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Grade of C or better in ENGR 217/PHYS 217 or PHYS 217/ENGR 217, or concurrent enrollment.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Students will be able to:
• Identify the importance of material properties and geometric attributes in manufacturing and design
• Understand the fundamentals of wide range of manufacturing methods
• Develop hands-on experiences with manufacturing processes and related skills in CAD, computer control, automation and simulation
• Evaluate multiple alternatives for product realization from the perspectives of economic criteria, manufacturability and performance / reliability

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
Recommended (and optional) textbook: Fundamentals of Modern Manufacturing: Materials, Processes and Systems, Mikell P. Groover, 7th edition, Wiley, ISBN:978-1-11-947521-7, 2019

Course Website: The course will use Canvas (https://canvas.tamu.edu/). Please log into the system using your NetID username and password and make sure you are able to access the course website. Please also check this website regularly, as it will be used to post announcements, quizzes, exams, lab assignments, and any other course material or information. Any information posted on the course website will be treated as if it is announced in the class and you are responsible to be aware of it.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignments / Quizzes: 20% (approximately 8 assignments and 25 quizzes)
Exams: 45% (3 exams, 15% each)
Lab attendance / assignments: 35%
Grades A for 90%-100%, B for 80%-89.99%, C for 70%-79.99%, D for 60%-69.99%, and F for <60%.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Missing an exam: If an exam is missed, you must have a written and authorized excuse that meets university excused absence requirements to qualify for a make-up exam. Wherever possible, students should inform the instructor before the exam. If not possible, the students must notify the instructor by the end of the second working day after missing an exam. Otherwise, they forfeit their rights to a make-up. Make-up exams will be given in accordance with University Rules (see Rule 7 at http://student-rules.tamu.edu/).

Missing a quiz: There is no make-up for missed quizzes. It is your responsibility to take the quizzes during the allocated time duration. Any student may drop two lowest quiz grades. If you miss a quiz due to an excused absence, your lowest grade (zero in this case) can be dropped, but no one can drop more than two grades.

Missing a lab: There is no make-up for missed labs. It is your responsibility to attend the lab and complete the assignment. However, if you miss a lab due to an excused absence, your lowest lab assignment grade (zero in this case) will be dropped and your average grade will be based on the remaining labs. You may not submit a lab assignment for points without attending the lab in person. Lab assignment submissions received after the due date will not be accepted or graded.

Important notes regarding excused absence:
The absence note must:
• Be in writing.
• Come from an authorized entity / authority.
• Clearly indicate the exact dates on which you are allowed to miss class/quiz/lab and state the reason. For example, just coming with a printed appointment from Student Health Services is not sufficient. According to student rules, “an absence for a non-acute medical service does not constitute an excused absence." If you were absent due to a serious or contagious condition, you should provide the instructor with a medical confirmation from Student Health Services (be aware that the Student Health Service does not provide notes retroactively). The note must indicate that you are excused from attending class on a specific date and state the reason unless it violates FERPA rules: http://registrar.tamu.edu/Catalogs,-Policies-Procedures/FERPA/FERPA-Notice-to- Students.

The instructor reserves their right to verify documentation and decline what they do not deem as a legitimate university excused absence.

chunks.title: Re-grading Policy
chunks.content: Any disagreements regarding a grade received on any graded material must be discussed within two business days of the return of the graded material. No grade will be changed beyond two business days.

chunks.title: Artificial Intelligence Statements
chunks.content: There are two recommended syllabus statement options for instructions considering adding a statement about the use of Artificial Intelligence in their course. These are located on the Teach with AI webpage hosted at ai.tamu.edu. Faculty are strongly encouraged to have a written policy on AI use in their course to avoid any confusion regarding what is or is not permitted.
